title: Partial updates without a framework
excerpt: A new browser primitive does htmx-style swaps with almost no JavaScript. Here is the measured catch.

title: The browser grew up
excerpt: The platform now does natively what we once reached for frameworks to do.

title: Measuring what the user actually downloads
excerpt: The only performance number that isn't a guess: bytes over the wire.

title: What a build step really costs
excerpt: A build step is never free — you just stop seeing the invoice.

title: Zero dependencies by default
excerpt: Every dependency is a bet on someone else's roadmap. Start from none.

title: A JS budget you can defend
excerpt: Set a byte budget first, then decide what earns a slot inside it.

title: Islands, hydration, and native
excerpt: Islands were a smart retreat toward the thing the platform already gives you.

title: SEO is a parity floor, not a feature
excerpt: Good metadata isn't a differentiator — it's the price of admission.

title: When a framework earns its JS
excerpt: Frameworks aren't the enemy — unearned framework JS is.

title: The cost of node_modules you never see
excerpt: The bytes that never ship to users still cost you supply chain and time.

title: Server-render first, enhance second
excerpt: Render the page on the server; add behavior only where it pays for itself.
